messages.content:
You are an expert at named entity recognition. Given an input text, extract all named entities along with their types and surrounding context.
Do not extract nested entities, only the outermost ones.

IMPORTANT: If an entity appears multiple times, but with different surrounding context, extract each occurrence separately.

The possible labels for named entities are:
PER: names of people (different languages, nicknames, usernames, fictional characters, titles with names, etc.)
LOC: names of cities, countries, landmarks, geographical features, addresses, etc.
ORG: names of companies, institutions, agencies, teams, etc.
MISC: everythin else that could be considered a named entity.

The order of labeling is PER, LOC, ORG, MISC.

Output format:
Return a JSON array of objects:
[
    { "entity": "ENTITY", "label": "ENTITY_LABEL", "context": "SURROUNDING_CONTEXT" },
    ...
]

Rules:
- "entity" must exactly match the original substring from the input text.
- "label" must be one of the specified entity labels.
- "context" must be a short snippet (3-6 words) from the input text that contains the entity and a few neighboring words.
- If the entity is at the beginning or end of the text, use only the available neighboring words.
- If there are no named entities, output an empty JSON array [].

Example:
Input text: "Barack Obama was born in Hawaii. Barack was american."
Output:
[
    { "entity": "Barack Obama", "label": "PER", "context": "Barack Obama was born" },
    { "entity": "Hawaii", "label": "LOC", "context": "born in Hawaii." },
    { "entity": "Barack", "label": "PER", "context": "Barack was american." }
]

IMPORTANT: Only output the JSON array. DO NOT add any explanations.

Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378. He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year. He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side; he emphasized the latter due to his lifelong affinity for the Bohemian side of his inheritance, and also because his direct ancestors in the Přemyslid line included two saints.[3][4]

He was the eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg, who died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346. His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia, was the sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland, the last of the male Přemyslid rulers of Bohemia. Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia.

<think>
Okay, let's tackle this named entity recognition task. The input is a historical text about Charles IV. First, I need to go through the text and identify all the named entities, making sure to categorize them correctly as PER, LOC, ORG, or MISC. 

Starting with the first sentence: "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1]), also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2] was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378." 

Here, "Charles IV" is clearly a person, so PER. The context would be the part around it, like "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1])". Then "Holy Roman Emperor" is an organization, ORG. The next part mentions "Charles of Luxembourg" – that's another PER. The name "Wenceslaus" is also a person, PER, with the context including the German and Czech names.

Moving on: "He was elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346 and became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year." "King of Germany" and "King of the Romans" are both ORG. "King of Bohemia" is another ORG. "Charles I" is a person, PER.

Next paragraph: "He was a member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side and the Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side..." "House of Luxembourg" and "Bohemian House of Přemyslid" are both ORG. 

Then: "John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia and Count of Luxembourg..." "John of Bohemia" is PER. "King of Bohemia" and "Count of Luxembourg" are ORG. "Battle of Crécy" is a LOC. 

In the next sentence: "His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia..." "Elizabeth" is PER, "Queen of Bohemia" is ORG. "Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland..." "Wenceslaus III" is PER, "King of Bohemia and Poland" is ORG. 

The last part: "Charles inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father and was elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia. On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia." "County of Luxembourg" is LOC, "Kingdom of Bohemia" is ORG. "Charles" is PER again.

I need to make sure not to miss any entities and check for duplicates with different contexts. For example, "Charles" appears multiple times but with different contexts. Each occurrence should be a separate entry. Also, dates and numbers are not entities here, so ignore them. 

Let me list all the entities I found:

- Charles IV (PER)
- Holy Roman Emperor (ORG)
- Charles of Luxembourg (PER)
- Wenceslaus (PER)
- King of Germany (ORG)
- King of the Romans (ORG)
- King of Bohemia (ORG)
- Charles I (PER)
- House of Luxembourg (ORG)
- Bohemian House of Přemyslid (ORG)
- John of Bohemia (PER)
- Battle of Crécy (LOC)
- Elizabeth (PER)
- Queen of Bohemia (ORG)
- Wenceslaus III (PER)
- King of Bohemia and Poland (ORG)
- County of Luxembourg (LOC)
- Kingdom of Bohemia (ORG)
- Charles (PER)

Now, check the context for each. For example, "Charles IV" is in the context "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1])". The context should be a snippet of 3-6 words around the entity. 

For "Holy Roman Emperor", the context is "was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378." Taking the first few words: "was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355".

Similarly, "Charles of Luxembourg" is in "also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2]".

Each entity's context should be a short snippet. Need to make sure that each occurrence is captured even if the same entity appears in different contexts. For example, "Charles" appears multiple times, each with different contexts.

I think that's all. Now, compiling them into the JSON array as specified, ensuring the entity matches exactly, the label is correct, and the context is a short snippet.
</think>

[
    { "entity": "Charles IV", "label": "PER", "context": "Charles IV (German: Karl IV.; Czech: Karel IV.; Latin: Carolus IV; 14 May 1316 – 29 November 1378[1])" },
    { "entity": "Holy Roman Emperor", "label": "ORG", "context": "was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378." },
    { "entity": "Charles of Luxembourg", "label": "PER", "context": "also known as Charles of Luxembourg, born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2]" },
    { "entity": "Wenceslaus", "label": "PER", "context": "born Wenceslaus (German: Wenzel, Czech: Václav),[2]" },
    { "entity": "King of Germany", "label": "ORG", "context": "elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346" },
    { "entity": "King of the Romans", "label": "ORG", "context": "elected King of Germany (King of the Romans) in 1346" },
    { "entity": "King of Bohemia", "label": "ORG", "context": "became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year." },
    { "entity": "Charles I", "label": "PER", "context": "became King of Bohemia (as Charles I) that same year." },
    { "entity": "House of Luxembourg", "label": "ORG", "context": "member of the House of Luxembourg from his father's side" },
    { "entity": "Bohemian House of Přemyslid", "label": "ORG", "context": "Bohemian House of Přemyslid from his mother's side" },
    { "entity": "John of Bohemia", "label": "PER", "context": "eldest son and heir of John of Bohemia, King of Bohemia" },
    { "entity": "Battle of Crécy", "label": "LOC", "context": "died at the Battle of Crécy on 26 August 1346." },
    { "entity": "Elizabeth", "label": "PER", "context": "His mother, Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia," },
    { "entity": "Queen of Bohemia", "label": "ORG", "context": "Elizabeth, Queen of Bohemia," },
    { "entity": "Wenceslaus III", "label": "PER", "context": "sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland," },
    { "entity": "King of Bohemia and Poland", "label": "ORG", "context": "sister of Wenceslaus III, King of Bohemia and Poland," },
    { "entity": "County of Luxembourg", "label": "LOC", "context": "inherited the County of Luxembourg from his father" },
    { "entity": "Kingdom of Bohemia", "label": "ORG", "context": "elected king of the Kingdom of Bohemia." },
    { "entity": "Charles", "label": "PER", "context": "On 2 September 1347, Charles was crowned King of Bohemia." }
]
